name: Visualize a Workflow Definition
query: Show me a visual diagram of my order-processing workflow
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is available
Step 2: Fetch the workflow definition using `conductor workflow get order-processing`
Step 3: Parse the workflow tasks to understand the flow structure
Step 4: Identify constructs: sequential tasks, SWITCH branches, FORK_JOIN parallel paths, DO_WHILE loops, WAIT/HUMAN tasks
Step 5: Generate a Mermaid flowchart using `flowchart TD` or `flowchart LR`
Step 6: Map each task to the correct Mermaid node shape (diamond for SWITCH, parallelogram for WAIT, etc.)
Step 7: Present the Mermaid diagram in a fenced code block
Step 8: Provide a brief text summary of the workflow flow alongside the diagram
success_criteria: Plan includes a command to fetch the workflow definition from the server (e.g., conductor workflow get order-processing) before generating the diagram
Mermaid diagram uses only safe syntax: flowchart TD/LR, --> arrows, no style/title/classDef
SWITCH tasks render as diamond nodes with labeled case edges
FORK_JOIN tasks show parallel branches converging at a join
WAIT/HUMAN tasks use parallelogram shape to indicate external input
Node labels include task type and reference name
Diagram is wrapped in a ```mermaid fenced code block
Text summary accompanies the diagram for accessibility
